# Codex math gate — is POLARIS full-power cost-per-run SOLID, or guessing?

ADVERSARIAL §-1.1 cost auditor. The operator demands REAL, SOLID math — "if we don't have solid math, nothing
works." A workflow produced the cost table below (its sub-agents may have leaked a forbidden advisor tool — do
NOT trust their self-checks; re-verify against the repo + live prices yourself). Rule whether the per-run math
is SOLID. Verify the LOAD-BEARING numbers file:line and web-check the prices. Output YAML verdict FIRST. iter 1.

```yaml
verdict: SOLID | NEEDS_CORRECTION | NOT_SOLID_YET
wrong_or_unverifiable_numbers: [...]
internal_contradictions: [...]
arithmetic_errors: [...]
the_measured_anchor_is: "<$0.064 or $0.7477 — which is the real shallow-run cost, and is the ContextVar-undercount claim true?>"
gpu_topology_correct: <true|false>   # does command-a-plus actually need 9xH100+1xA100?
expected_cost_per_run: "<your own grounded number or band, both amortization models>"
is_it_solid_enough_to_act_on: <true|false>   # or must the operator buy ONE warm run first?
honest_one_line: "<for the operator>"
```

## The math to verify (full arithmetic in outputs/I-meta-002/cost_synthesis_full_power_per_run.md — READ IT)
MEASURED ANCHOR (the 12x correction): workflow claims the shallow tirzepatide run cost $0.7477 (re-summed
logs/pg_cost_ledger.jsonl, 138 entries = $0.7371 generate + $0.0106 entailment_judge), NOT the $0.064 in
manifest.json — claiming manifest cost_usd is a per-asyncio-task ContextVar UNDER-count. VERIFY: filter the
ledger to THIS run's entries and sum; read the cost-tracking code to confirm/refute the ContextVar-undercount
claim. This is the anchor everything scales from — it MUST be right.
INTERNAL CONTRADICTION to rule on: the file's read-aloud summary (§3) still says "six cents" while the footer
(§4) says $0.7477. Which is correct?
MODELS (config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml): generator deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro (hosted);
verifiers self-hosted = mirror cohere/command-a-plus, sentinel ibm-granite/granite-guardian-4.1-8b, judge
qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b. VERIFY command-a-plus actual parameter count + bf16 VRAM (workflow says 218B MoE, ~438GB
load) and whether that truly needs 9xH100+1xA100. (NOTE: earlier I told the operator "Command A 111B -> 2x80GB"
— that was the WRONG older model; the lock pins command-a-PLUS. Confirm the corrected sizing.)
GPU PRICE: Vast H100 $2.28/hr x9 + A100 $1.07/hr x1 = cluster $21.59/hr. Verify the arithmetic (9*2.28=20.52
+1.07=21.59) AND web-check Vast H100/A100 current hourly.
PER-RUN BANDS: Model A warm steady-state = $21.59/hr x wall-clock 1.17-1.54hr + API $0.90-1.79 = ~$26-35/run.
Model B dedicated-over-golden-5 = cold-start (box held 7.93-12.80hr) /5 = ~$35-57/run; batch ~$185-295.
API/token subtotal $0.90 sticker / $1.79 billed (Serper $0.216 + DeepSeek gen $0.442+$0.189 + gap $0.046 +
deepener $0.006). DeepSeek V4 Pro price $0.435/M in, $0.87/M out (openrouter.ai/deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro) —
web-verify. Generation tokens scaled gen-cap 20->50 (2.5x) + length x2.0 from the measured ledger.
CLINICALTRIALS.GOV: workflow says it fail-closes 403, not wired (domain_backends.py:452-453) — verify (a
currently-dead channel changes the "free clinical channels" story).

## The decisive question
The workflow's OWN biggest-uncertainty flag: the 8-round iterative loop is a TARGET the shipped code does NOT
execute (shipped = ONE main pass + <=4-query R6 gap), so the 70-92min full-power wall-clock is UNMEASURED; and
GPU is >90% of cost, so a 2x wall-clock error swings per-run from ~$15 to ~$60. The 4-role gate also has ZERO
measured token data. RULE: is the ~$30 warm / ~$45 dedicated figure SOLID enough for the operator to act on, or
is the honest answer "you cannot have solid per-run math until you buy ONE warm full-power run to measure
wall-clock + 4-role tokens"? Give your own grounded band and say plainly which it is.

.codex\I-meta-002\research_findings.json:153:      "Q1 (does clinical data touch US jurisdiction when running your own vLLM container on an EU-located GPU?): YES, residually \u2014 through TWO mechanisms, NOT through GPU physical location. .codex\I-meta-002\research_findings.json:194:    "recommendation": "REJECT one-size-fits-all (options a/b/c/d as a single choice). The highest-quality policy is VERDICT-SPECIFIC and TWO-AXIS, mirroring the already-frozen, Codex-approved verdict-severity ordering that POLARIS's scoring layer locked (reconcile.py: VERIFIED<PARTIAL<UNREACHABLE<UNSUPPORTED<FABRICATED, conservative-MAX; claim_audit_scorer.py: FABRICATED+UNSUPPORTED=hard_fail, PARTIAL=0.5, UNREACHABLE full-weight-but-not-hard-fail, S3 observe-only). The release policy must reuse this ordering, not invent a parallel one. Split the decision into (i) claim-action and (ii) report-gate (release_allowed). FABRICATED(material) = occurrence-gate (refuse-in-place AND release_allowed=false even after removal \u2014 fabrication is an active falsehood + generator-integrity signal). UNSUPPORTED(material) = residual-gate (rewrite one pass; if still unsupported, refuse-in-place; release_allowed=false only if post-removal Lane-2 material coverage drops below the locked 0.70 threshold). PARTIAL / UNREACHABLE = soft (caveat-and-keep with visible verdict marker, release_allowed stays true). Gate on ANY material (S0-S2) hard-fail with ZERO tolerance \u2014 never a count/rate threshold (that is \u00a7-1.1-banned metadata AND resurrects the BUG-M-205 \"after three failures\" anti-pattern the code already fixed). The competitive differentiator is the per-claim verdict ledger + transparent machine-checkable gap-list, NOT blanket suppression (\"we only show verified claims\" is the WRONG framing \u2014 the literature recommends systematic verification over abstention). Drop never means silent deletion: refuse-in-place per the atom_refusal_validator STRICT precedent + gaps.json sidecar (LAW II fail-loudly). release_allowed=false/abort still emits report.md as a reviewable verdict artifact (\u00a79.1 invariant 4) \u2014 it means \"gated from auto-release pending human sign-off,\" not \"no output.\"",

.codex\I-meta-002\feasibility_synthesis.md:47:- **Hardware (MoE â€” all 35B resident, ~3B active):** bf16 â‰ˆ 70 GB â†’ tight/OOM on one 80 GB H100 at 262K context (needs H200 141 GB or TP). **FP8 (`Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8`) â‰ˆ 35 GB â†’ fits one H100/H200 cleanly â€” recommended self-host target.** H100+ has FP8 tensor cores; **A100 lacks them â†’ use INT8 on A100.** INT4 â‰ˆ 21 GB fits a 24 GB GPU.

.codex\I-meta-002\feasibility_synthesis.md:60:**Why Vast.ai is forbidden for clinical (residual that survives ALL mitigations):** Vast.ai Inc. is **US-incorporated** (CA stock corp / DE parent) running a **privileged management daemon on each host** + the marketplace control plane. (SSH proxy-vs-direct is the *weakest* concern; direct SSH + own TLS defeats it but does NOT close the host-root / US-daemon exposure.)
.codex\I-meta-002\feasibility_synthesis.md:75:**Design proposal that EXTENDS the frozen scorer ordering** â€” it is NOT what the scorer currently does. .codex\I-meta-002\codex_design_iter1_verdict.txt:701:.\outputs\audits\I-bug-092\claude_audit.md:12:The 2026-05-09 line-by-line content audit (`outputs/audits/v32_baseline_content_audit/CROSS_REVIEW.md`) found that the V3.2-Exp + Gemma 4 31B baseline tirzepatide report has **1 fabricated mechanistic claim (M2)**, **1 specificity inflation (C2)**, and **1 unverifiable specificity claim (C1)** â€” all of which passed the existing 5 mechanical strict_verify checks (token presence, token validity, span bounds, decimal subset, content-word overlap).
.codex\I-meta-002\codex_design_iter1_verdict.txt:702:.\outputs\audits\I-bug-092\claude_audit.md:14:The architectural diagnosis: strict_verify enforces **provenance presence** (token format + lexical overlap), not **provenance correctness** (semantic entailment between sentence and span). The current design assumes lexical overlap implies content fidelity. Empirically with capable generators, this assumption breaks â€” they generate plausible-sounding extensions of the source's topic that share words but introduce unsourced facts.
.codex\I-meta-002\codex_design_iter1_verdict.txt:703:.\outputs\audits\I-bug-092\claude_audit.md:18:Adds check 6 to `strict_verify.verify_sentence()`: an LLM-as-judge entailment call that asks whether the cited span semantically ENTAILS the sentence's specific claims. Gated by `PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT={off,warn,enforce}` defaulting to `off` so production behavior is unchanged until an operator opts in. Default judge model is `google/gemma-4-31b-it` (the existing two-family evaluator); custom model via `PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL`.

docs\vast_ai_budget_i_meta_002.md:13:### 0.1 The Generator (DeepSeek V4 Pro) is NOT on Vast in any scenario
docs\vast_ai_budget_i_meta_002.md:14:I-meta-002 wires **three** new roles: Mirror, Sentinel, Judge. The Generator (DeepSeek V4 Pro, ~8Ã—H200, the largest model in the stack, 1M context) is **already running on OpenRouter API** in the current benchmark code (`pathB_run_gate.py` pins `OPENROUTER_PROVIDER_ORDER`, single provider e.g. `deepinfra`; the 16 smoke runs on 2026-05-28 used the API path). There is **no supplied Vast price for a V4-Pro-capable box**, and folding an 8Ã—H100 generator into a scenario would silently dominate every total. So:
docs\vast_ai_budget_i_meta_002.md:16:> **Generator stays on OpenRouter API across ALL THREE benchmark scenarios.** Its cost is a small token line, not a Vast GPU line. The *sovereign self-hosted* generator (8Ã—H200) belongs to **production line #4** (Phase-4-gated OVH-Canada/EU tranche per `docs/blockers.md`), NOT to this Vast benchmark budget.

docs\transparency.md:60:- `google.serper.dev` â€” Serper web search (US-based; see "Web search provider" below)
docs\transparency.md:68:**Web search provider:** Serper (`google.serper.dev`), a US-based search API. This is a deliberate, disclosed exception to the "no US company" posture, accepted per operator directive 2026-05-13.
docs\transparency.md:70:What Serper receives: the search query string itself, plus the normal request metadata any HTTP API call carries â€” the API account/key, source IP, timestamp, and user-agent â€” which Serper's privacy policy (serper.dev/privacy) describes it logging as system/access activity. What Serper does NOT receive: the uploaded corpus, the evidence pool, the generated report, or any operator-entered content. Serper returns only result URLs + snippets; POLARIS then fetches the actual T1 evidence directly from the government corpus endpoints listed above.
docs\transparency.md:72:The rationale for accepting the exception: POLARIS's sovereignty constraint protects the **LLM inference path and the generated report data**. The orchestrator + report data run on Canadian/non-US infrastructure (Vexxhost / OVH QuÃ©bec); the LLM inference path is *designed* for the sovereign OVH H200 vLLM endpoint but **currently routes via OpenRouter (US, transitional â€” see the egress entry above)** until the sovereign GPU cluster is brought up at the Session-A window. That transitional US dependency is the one inference-path exception, disclosed here and surfaced in-product. A web-search query is a short keyword string carrying no confidential research content; the sensitive artifact is the synthesized report, which never transits Serper. A reviewer who wants zero US touch in the search path can swap Serper for a non-US provider (Mojeek UK / Qwant FR / Ecosia DE) â€” the retrieval code is provider-shaped â€” but that is not required for the sovereignty posture as scoped.

docs\clinical_rag_validation_sota_2026_05_26.md:31:1. **Prompt-level atom injection.** V4 Pro (DeepSeek's reasoning model) is prompted to emit per-claim `atom_NNN` tokens inline with the generated text, plus `[ev_XXX]` evidence-block citations. docs\clinical_rag_validation_sota_2026_05_26.md:77:**Performance â€” and this is the critical number for POLARIS.** Lynx 70B beats **GPT-4o by 8.3% on PubMedQA medical hallucination detection** [^patronus_lynx_blog]. docs\clinical_rag_validation_sota_2026_05_26.md:79:**Sovereignty fit.** **Excellent.** Open-source, Llama-3 derivative (Llama-3 community license â€” commercially usable for orgs <700M MAU, POLARIS qualifies). docs\clinical_rag_validation_sota_2026_05_26.md:113:**MiniCheck (the production-ready option).** 770M Flan-T5-Large, sentence-level fact-checker, **matches GPT-4 on LLM-AggreFact (74.7% vs 75.3%, 0.6% gap) at 400Ã— lower cost** [^minicheck_2024]. Available on GitHub. **However: the paper explicitly states "we disregard the usual 'contradiction' class from textual entailment, as contradictions are rare in our benchmark"** â€” this means MiniCheck will NOT reliably catch the constipation negation fabrication, because it treats faithfulness as binary support/no-support, not three-way support/neutral/contradiction. **Disqualifier for POLARIS clinical use case.**
docs\clinical_rag_validation_sota_2026_05_26.md:115:**MedScore (the medical-specific decomposition).** Specifically designed for medical free-form text. Could be re-implemented with a local Qwen3 / DeepSeek-distilled judge.
docs\clinical_rag_validation_sota_2026_05_26.md:137:**What it does.** Use a strong LLM (Claude Opus 4.7, GPT-5, Gemini 2.5 Pro, Llama 3.3 70B) as a judge, prompted to do per-claim faithfulness verification with quoted evidence. Datadog's 2025 engineering blog and Patronus's research both validate this as the highest-quality detection method when no fine-tuned alternative exists [^datadog_judge_2025]. Lynx (Â§3.3) is the open-source instantiation of this pattern, pre-trained for it.

docs\clinical_rag_sota_deepest_research_2026_05_27.md:21:1. **Generator: Qwen3-235B-A22B (Apache 2.0) or DeepSeek-V3.2-Exp (MIT)**, NOT DeepSeek V4 Pro. docs\clinical_rag_sota_deepest_research_2026_05_27.md:24:4. **Outer information-theoretic ensemble gate: a CHECK-style log-probability classifier across an ensemble of 3-5 open-weight LLMs.** This is the new dominant finding in this round of research â€” the CHECK paper (Moffitt Cancer Center + UCSF, June 2025) reduces Llama3.3-70B-Instruct hallucination rate **from 31% to 0.3% on 1,500 clinical trial questions** by using a stacked classifier on entropy + KL-divergence features from a 5-LLM ensemble (LLaMA 3.1-8B + 3.1-70B + 3.3-70B + Nemotron-70B + DeepSeek-V1) [^check_paper]. Operationalised as BlueScrubs platform; no public GitHub yet but architecture is fully described in the paper and re-implementable.
docs\clinical_rag_sota_deepest_research_2026_05_27.md:28:**The single most expensive belief POLARIS is currently holding that this research overturns:** the operator-locked DeepSeek V4 Pro generator is the WRONG generator for the hallucination-sensitivity dimension. Vectara HHEM-2.3 evaluator (May 2026 leaderboard) ranks V4 Pro at 8.6%, V3.2-Exp at 5.3%, Qwen3-8B at 4.8%, Antgroup Finix S1 32B at 1.8%. All four lower-hallucination options are open-weight non-US. V4 Pro is also notorious for 94% non-abstention on AA-Omniscience (it answers when it doesn't know). The 44.4% refusal rate POLARIS is seeing is V4 Pro's low-abstention pathology being correctly caught by POLARIS's validator. This is a generator-choice problem, not a validator problem. **Per CLAUDE.md Â§-1.1 + `feedback_route_policy_questions_to_codex.md` I am surfacing this for Codex review, not silently switching.** Operator's "frontier-only, top-notch only" directive was a general capability directive; the dimension-specific data argues for an additional consideration. Most defensible path: keep V4 Pro as primary reasoning generator AND add Qwen3-8B as a parallel "low-hallucination prose finalizer" model in the same ensemble used by CHECK, exploiting the family diversity that POLARIS's two-family invariant (Â§9.1) already requires.
docs\clinical_rag_sota_deepest_research_2026_05_27.md:52:4. **No clinical-database grounding layer separate from the per-document evidence pool.** POLARIS grounds against the corpus the operator provides per-run. CHECK grounds against a continuously-updated ClinicalTrials.gov-derived database (68k trials) maintained at BlueScrubs. The two are complementary: per-run corpus catches in-context contradictions; persistent database catches global contradictions (the "this drug isn't approved for that indication" class). **Fix:** ship a ClinicalTrials.gov mirror as a sovereign reference database (the data is public-domain US-government data, mirroring it is unrestricted, sovereignty story holds because we host the mirror).

docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:115:### 3.1 DeepSeek V3.2-Exp
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:119:**Cons.** Smaller HLE score than V4 Pro (21.7 vs 37.7 thinking mode) â€” V3.2 is a less capable reasoner. "Experimental" tag means DeepSeek may deprecate or replace before Carney delivery. SuperGPQA, LongGenBench, calibration, citation-existence: all UNKNOWN.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:121:**Latest issues.** None major from HF/GitHub trending. The DeepSeek-V3.2-Exp [arxiv paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.02556) is the primary source.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:123:**Community sentiment.** Treated as the most-trustworthy DeepSeek for RAG (low Vectara halluc), but seen as a stop-gap before V4 stable.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:125:### 3.2 DeepSeek V4 Pro
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:131:**Latest issues.** [DeepSeek V4 Pro Hugging Face page](https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro) â€” Day-0 support across all major inference frameworks confirmed. No CVEs / safety incidents reported as of 2026-05-27.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:137:**Pros.** Apache 2.0 â€” cleanest commercial license. Active 22B param footprint â†’ cheaper inference than V4 Pro at comparable scale. SuperGPQA family-leader (Qwen3.5-397B at 0.704; Qwen3.6+ at 0.716). MMLU-ProX leader (Qwen3.5-397B at 84.7, Qwen3.6 Plus at 84.7). 100+ languages including strong FR. FinanceBench Capability leader (73.0 via Qwen3-32B sibling). AA-Omniscience: Qwen3.7 Max hallucination rate 22.9% (lowest in frontier group, down from 44.2% on Qwen3.6).
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:149:**Pros.** Apache 2.0. **Best Vectara hallucination rates** of the candidates (8B = 4.8%, 14B = 5.4%, 32B = 5.9% â€” lower than DeepSeek V3.2-Exp at 5.3%; 8B is the LOWEST of the entire open-weight non-US set). Strong answer rates (99.9%). Dense â†’ easy to deploy at single-H100 (32B INT4) or single-T4 (8B). GPQA Diamond: 32B = 62.1, 14B = 59.1, 8B = 62.0 (size-saturated by ~8B). 32B is BFCL v3 strong (75.7).
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:167:### 3.6 Llama 3.3 70B
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:169:**Pros.** **Best IFEval score in the candidate set (92.1)** â€” strongest instruction-follower. Vectara HHEM-2.3 = 4.1% (best dense-model number). 99.5% answer rate. Mature inference everywhere. Family C (Meta) gives strongest two-family diversity vs DeepSeek + Qwen.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:171:**Cons.** **License is the dealbreaker for Carney**: Llama Community License has (a) a 700M MAU clause that converts the licence to "Meta's sole discretion" beyond that scale [^llama_lic], (b) a competitor restriction blocking entire industries, (c) a ban on using Llama to train other models â€” which restricts the CHECK-style ensemble distillation pattern POLARIS uses. Even for Carney delivery (one-shot gift), the trained-other-model clause may bind if POLARIS uses any Llama-derived distillation. **Sovereignty concern**: Meta is US-origin. While the WEIGHTS can be self-hosted in Canada, the license is Meta-controlled and the policy is US-export-regulated for some destinations. MMLU-Pro 68.9 â€” weakest reasoning of the frontier candidates. GPQA Diamond 50.5.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:173:**Latest issues.** [Llama 3.3 70B official page](https://www.llama.com/llama3_3/license/) â€” the 700M MAU clause is in the license verbatim. Llama 4 family released in 2026 with similar terms.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:179:**Pros.** **MIT license** â€” same permissiveness as DeepSeek. Tsinghua University spinoff backed by Alibaba + Tencent â†’ Chinese-origin frontier. GPQA = 82.9 with tools (essentially tied with Claude 4.5 at 83.4). 8-benchmark panel includes AIME 25, GPQA, LCB v6, HLE, SWE-Bench. BFCL v3 4.5 = 76.7 (top-of-leaderboard among open-weights). vLLM/SGLang/TRT-LLM Day-0 support.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:181:**Cons.** Vectara HHEM-2.3 = **9.5% halluc rate, 94.5% answer rate** â€” second-worst of the candidates (only Qwen3-235B-A22B at 9.3% is comparable). MMLU-Pro UNKNOWN for 4.6 explicitly (4.5 = 84.6). Less stable maintenance cadence than DeepSeek / Qwen.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:207:**Qwen3-32B / Llama 3.3 70B / GLM-4.6 as LLM-judge**: large LLMs used as judges via prompt engineering. Strengths inherit from the generator scores above. Weakness: judge-as-LLM still emits free-form CoT; Codex's revised schema (label + score + evidence_span + contradiction_span + abstain_reason) is enforceable via grammar but per-sentence latency is slow vs classifier verifiers.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:215:The single most striking pattern across the data: **hallucination and reasoning are NEGATIVELY correlated within DeepSeek's open-weight family**. V4 Pro is the strongest reasoner (37.7 HLE; 90.1 GPQA) BUT has the highest Vectara hallucination rate (8.6%). V3.2-Exp loses ~15 HLE points but cuts hallucination by ~40% (5.3%). Qwen3-8B has the LOWEST hallucination (4.8%) but cannot reason at frontier level.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:221:Apache 2.0 (Qwen3, Mistral Large 3) and MIT (DeepSeek V4 Pro, V3.2-Exp, GLM-4.6) are tied for cleanest commercial usability. Llama Community (Llama 3.3 70B) is the weakest license among capable candidates â€” the 700M MAU clause is forward-looking risk but the "ban on training other models" clause is immediate risk for any ensemble distillation. CC-BY-NC-4.0 (Aloe Beta 72B, Patronus Lynx) is commercially DISQUALIFIED.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:238:- RULER 128K per-model (only DeepSeek-V3 paper reports it; Qwen3 reports it differently; Llama 3.3 isn't on the NoLiMa leaderboard)
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:246:The clear pattern: **classifier verifiers (HHEM-2.1-Open, VerifAI DeBERTa) win on speed + structured output reliability**; LLM-judges (Qwen3-32B, Llama 3.3 70B, GLM-4.6) win on reasoning depth + explanation quality but at 10-100x the latency cost. Patronus Lynx is the published-best LLM-judge but is commercially DISQUALIFIED.

1: # POLARIS FULL-POWER COST PER RUN — synthesis
2: 
3: Single per-run cost model, reconciled from five grounded component analyses.
4: Measured anchor: `outputs/honest_sweep_r3/clinical/clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm/`
5: (shallow run, gen-cap 20, verifier off-GPU, deepener off, cost ledger = $0.748 total).
6: Every line below is [M]easured (read from the real run / ledger / config) or
7: [P]rojected (scaled to full-power with arithmetic shown).
8: 
9: ---
10: 
11: ## 1. Per-run line-item table (de-duplicated)
12: 
13: | Stage / Tool | Unit price (source) | Units per full-power run | $ per run | M/P |
14: |---|---|---|---|---|
15: | **Serper web search** | $1.00 / 1k (entry); $0.30/1k at scale — serper.dev | 216 calls = 41 round-1 [M `manifest.retrieval.api_calls.serper`] + 7×25 gap rounds [P] | **$0.216** (entry) / $0.065 (scale) | P |
16: | **Semantic Scholar** (search + deepener) | $0.00 free — semanticscholar.org/product/api | 216 search + 56 deepener S2 calls | $0.000 | M (free, code-confirmed) |
17: | **PubMed E-utilities / Europe PMC** | $0.00 free — ncbi.nlm.nih.gov | ≤32 calls (4/round × 8) | $0.000 | M (free) |
18: | **ClinicalTrials.gov** | $0.00 free | 0 — fail-closed 403, not wired (`domain_backends.py:452-453`) | $0.000 | M (does not fire) |
19: | **Generation — section writers + outline** (DeepSeek V4 Pro, hosted OpenRouter) | $0.435/M in · $0.87/M out — openrouter.ai/deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro | in 623,305 (=249,322×50/20) · out 103,188 · rsn 93,010 (×2.0 length) | **$0.442** (sticker) / $1.261 (billed) | P, scaled from [M] ledger |
20: | **Generation — gap-fill / synthesis / regen / limitations / trial-table** (DeepSeek V4 Pro) | same | in 44,948 (×1.3) · out 103,876 · rsn 90,334 (×2.0) | **$0.189** / $0.368 | P |
21: | **Gap-analysis — 8-round iterative query-gen** (DeepSeek V4 Pro) | same | 16 calls (2/round × 8): in 12,800 · out 24,000 · rsn 22,400 | **$0.046** / $0.091 | P |
22: | **Deepener LLM calls** (DeepSeek V4 Pro, OP-1 + OP-5) | same | 2 calls: OP-1 ~9k in/1.5k out + OP-5 ~1.5k in/0.6k out | **$0.0064** | P (anchored to [M] call profile) |
23: | **Verifier — Mirror** (Cohere Command A+ 218B MoE, self-hosted GPU) | GPU $/hr line below — no token $ | 300 calls (2/claim × 150); 496,875 tok; 127.2 GPU-s | (in GPU line) | P |
24: | **Verifier — Sentinel** (Granite Guardian 8B, self-hosted GPU) | GPU $/hr line below | 150 calls; 403,125 tok; 18.4 GPU-s | (in GPU line) | P |

27: | **Embeddings / KG / signing / storage** (self-hosted MiniLM, local Chroma, local GPG) | $0.00 — self-hosted/local, code-confirmed | MiniLM-L6 embeds ~190 rows; local KG writes; GPG sign | $0.000 | M (self-hosted) |
28: 
29: ### De-duplication notes (where two agents overlapped — corrected)
30: 1. **Legacy `entailment_judge` ($0.0106, 107 calls) is NOT charged.** The ledger's
31:    entailment_judge line is the **legacy 2-LLM Qwen evaluator**, replaced by the
32:    self-hosted 4-role GPU gate in full-power. Counting it would double-bill verification.
33:    It belongs to neither full-power line. (Confirmed: ledger total $0.7477 = $0.7371 generate + $0.0106 entailment_judge.)
34: 2. **Gap-analysis tokens** appear once only — on the Generation/Gap line (DeepSeek hosted),
35:    NOT on the verifier GPU line. The 4-role gate scores *kept verified sentences*, not gap queries.
36: 3. **Search calls** are counted once on the Serper line. The deepener's S2 calls are free
37:    and listed separately; the deepener's *extra-fetched rows'* downstream gen/verify cost
38:    is already captured by the gen-cap-50 scaling (not re-added).
39: 4. **Verifier token cost = $0 OpenRouter** (self-hosted); its real cost is GPU-seconds,
40:    folded into the single GPU serving wall-clock line, not billed per-token.
41: 
42: ---
43: 
44: ## 2. Per-run total — both amortization models
45: 
46: **Token/API costs (Serper + DeepSeek generation + gap + deepener), summed:**
47: - Sticker-rate floor (OpenRouter posted): 0.216 + 0.442 + 0.189 + 0.046 + 0.0064 = **$0.90/run**
48: - Measured-billed-rate (run's observed 2.51× provider-mix premium): 0.065 + 1.261 + 0.368 + 0.091 + 0.0064 = **$1.79/run** (Serper at scale tier)
49: - API band: **$0.90 – $1.79 / run**
50: 
51: **GPU serving is the dominant cost. Two amortization models:**
52: 
53: ### MODEL A — warm steady-state ($/run = cluster $/hr × wall-clock)
54: Marginal cost of run N on an already-warm, long-lived cluster.
55: 
56: | | LOW | EXPECTED | HIGH |
57: |---|---|---|---|
58: | GPU (Vast $21.59/hr × wall-clock) | $25.19 (1.17 hr) | $29.00 (1.34 hr) | $33.25 (1.54 hr) |
59: | API/token | $0.90 | $1.35 | $1.79 |
60: | **Warm $/run** | **≈ $26** | **≈ $30** | **≈ $35** |
61: 
62: (EU-sovereign uplift: OVH $31.14/hr → ≈ $37–49/run; Scaleway $28.35/hr → ≈ $34–45/run.)
63: 
64: ### MODEL B — dedicated rental amortized over the golden-5 batch
65: Cold-start (Mirror 438 GB load + 8-GPU bringup + debug + teardown → box held 7.93–12.80 hr) paid once, ÷5.
66: 
67: | | LOW | EXPECTED | HIGH |
68: |---|---|---|---|
69: | GPU (Vast, box-hold ÷5) | $34 | $44 | $55 |
70: | API/token | $0.90 | $1.35 | $1.79 |
71: | **Rental-over-5 $/run** | **≈ $35** | **≈ $45** | **≈ $57** |
72: 
73: (Whole golden-5 batch ≈ $185–295 on Vast. EU-sovereign: OVH ≈ $50–81/run; Scaleway ≈ $45–74/run.)
74: 
75: ---
76: 
77: ## 3. Plain-language summary (for a blind operator, read aloud)
78: 
79: The dominant cost by far is renting the GPU box to host the three self-hosted
80: verifier models — the LLM tokens are pocket change next to it. If the server is
81: already warm and you just run one more question, it's about thirty dollars a run.
82: If you rent a box only to prove the five golden questions and then tear it down,
83: the cold-start setup time spreads over five runs and pushes it to about forty-five
84: dollars a run, roughly two hundred to three hundred dollars for the whole batch.
85: The only fully measured number is the cheap shallow run at six cents; everything
86: above it is projected from real vendor prices I looked up live and from the real
87: configured caps in the code — no guessed dollar figures.
88: 
89: ---
90: 
91: ## 4. HONESTY FOOTER — measured vs projected, biggest uncertainty
92: 
93: **[MEASURED] (read from the real run / ledger / config):**
94: - Shallow-run total cost $0.7477 from `cost_ledger.jsonl` (138 entries; $0.7371 generate / $0.0106 entailment_judge) — verified by independent sum this session.
95: - Manifest `cost_usd` $0.064 is a per-asyncio-task ContextVar UNDER-count, not the run's real cost.
96: - Search call counts (serper 41, s2 41) from `manifest.retrieval.api_calls`.
97: - All caps/model pins: gen-cap 20, fetch-cap 40, DeepSeek V4 Pro hosted via OpenRouter,
98:   4-role lock (Cohere Command A+ 218B / Granite 8B / Qwen 3.6-35B-A3B), self-hosted embeddings/Chroma/GPG.
99: - Verifier CALL STRUCTURE (Mirror 2/claim, Sentinel 1/claim, Judge 1/claim) read from adapters.
100: - Evidence-row sizes (~1,264 tok/row) from `evidence_pool.json`.
101: - Shallow-run wall-clock 27.5 min.
102: - Frontier targets: ChatGPT Q1 4,830 w / ~45 cites; Gemini Q1 6,835 w / 50 works cited (measured via wc on stored compare files).
103: 
104: **[PROJECTED] (scaled to full-power with arithmetic shown — every line above marked P):**
105: - 8-round loop firing fresh 25-query batteries (shipped code does ONE main pass + ≤4-query R6 gap pass; the 8-round loop is a TARGET, not shipped behavior).
106: - Gen-cap 20→50 (2.5×) token scaling; report length ×2.0.
107: - Claim count 150 (band 95–250) for the 4-role gate.
108: - All verifier per-role token figures and GPU-seconds (4-role gate has ZERO measured token data — was not live on the measured run).
109: - GPU-throughput rates (prefill 30k tok/s, Mirror decode 375 tok/s, Sentinel 150, Judge 200) — web-searched, not measured on this hardware.
110: - Full-power wall-clock 1.17–1.54 hr; cold-start 1.6–2.6 hr.
111: - Deepener 2-call cost (deepener was OFF on the measured run).
112: - Effective billed rate $1.538/M (2.51× sticker) — measured on the shallow run, projected to apply at scale.
113: 
114: **SINGLE BIGGEST UNCERTAINTY:** the **GPU wall-clock per full-power run** (and the
115: cold-start setup hours), because (a) the 8-round iterative loop is a TARGET that the
116: shipped code does NOT execute, so the 70–92 min runtime is an unmeasured projection,
117: and (b) the GPU line is the dominant cost (>90%), so a 2× error in wall-clock swings
118: the per-run total from ~$15 to ~$60. The 4-role gate having zero measured token data
119: compounds this: its 6-minute GPU contribution is entirely projected from code call-structure.
120: A single warm full-power run on rented hardware would collapse this uncertainty — it is
121: the one measurement worth buying before committing to a benchmark budget.
122: 
123: **UNGROUNDED flags:** none. Every dollar figure cites a repo file:line or a live vendor
124: price URL. Where a component could not be measured (4-role tokens, 8-round loop), it is
125: labeled [PROJECTED] with the scaling arithmetic, not invented.

logs\pg_cost_ledger.jsonl:40090:{"timestamp": "2026-05-26T07:55:52.257395+00:00", "session_id": "SWEEP_clinical_clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm_1779781185", "call_type": "entailment_judge", "input_tokens": 556, "output_tokens": 34, "reas
 succeeded in 1553ms:
1: """
2: OpenRouter LLM Client for polaris graph.
3: 
4: Single gateway via OpenRouter. Default model = OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL
5: (default deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro per I-cd-009 / GH#624 Carney demo lock).
6: Two call modes:
7: - reason(): reasoning ON, returned separately in reasoning_details
8: - generate(): reasoning OFF, clean prose output only
9: 
10: No CoT scrubbing. No regex filters. No post-processing hacks.
11: The API handles separation at the protocol level.
12: """
13: 
14: import asyncio
15: import json
16: import logging
17: import os
18: import re
19: import time
20: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
21: from datetime import datetime, timezone
22: from pathlib import Path
23: from typing import Any, Optional, Type, TypeVar
24: 
25: import httpx
26: from dotenv import load_dotenv
27: from pydantic import BaseModel, ValidationError
28: 
29: from src.polaris_graph.tracing import _current_tracer
30: # I-safety-002b (#925): Path-B benchmark gate capture (stdlib-only, no circular import;
31: # all calls are gate-flagged via is_active() so the hot path pays one contextvar read).
32: from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture as _pathb_capture
33: 
34: load_dotenv()
35: 
36: logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
37: 
38: T = TypeVar("T", bound=BaseModel)
39: 
40: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41: # Configuration
42: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
43: 
44: # FIX-305: Persistent cost ledger
45: _COST_LEDGER_PATH = Path(os.getenv("PG_COST_LEDGER_PATH", "logs/pg_cost_ledger.jsonl"))
46: 
47: OPENROUTER_API_KEY = os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "")
48: OPENROUTER_BASE_URL = os.getenv(
49:     "OPENROUTER_BASE_URL", "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"
50: )
51: OPENROUTER_MODEL = os.getenv("OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro")
52: OPENROUTER_BUDGET_USD = float(os.getenv("OPENROUTER_BUDGET_USD", "50.0"))
53: 
54: # R-2 (readiness gate): hard per-run cost cap — a RUNAWAY-LOOP GUARD,
55: # not an economic limit. Measured by cumulative cost across ALL
56: # OpenRouterClient instances within a single run.
57: #
58: # I-gen-003 (2026-05-14): raised the default from $0.10 to $10.00.
59: # The $0.10 default was tuned for the V3.2-Exp generator
60: # ($0.005-$0.01/run). DeepSeek V4 Pro is reasoning-first — it emits
61: # ~3x the tokens (huge reasoning traces: ~5000 reasoning tokens/call
62: # observed) and the I-gen-003 CoT-recovery regen loop adds up to 3
63: # extra section calls. Under the stale $0.10 cap a normal V4 Pro run
64: # trips BudgetExceededError before it can finish — i.e. the guard
65: # false-fires on the new generator's NORMAL cost profile. $10.00
66: # matches the honest_sweep_job_runner.py default and still catches a genuine
67: # infinite loop / recursive-outline runaway. Override per-run via the
68: # PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN env var if a tighter ceiling is wanted.
69: PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN = float(os.getenv("PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN", "10.00"))
70: 
71: # BUG-B-201 fix (pass 2 remediation): per-task ambient state via
72: # contextvars so concurrent async run_one_query() calls don't stomp
73: # each other's run_id + cost accumulator.
74: #
75: # Pre-fix (commit f632ee5), `_CURRENT_RUN_ID` and `_RUN_COST_USD` were
76: # module-level globals. Pass 2 showed that under `asyncio.gather`,
77: # a later run_one_query overwrote the earlier run's ambient id before
78: # downstream OpenRouterClient() construction — tagging the first run's
79: # LLM calls with the second run's id.
80: #
81: # Post-fix: ContextVar scopes per asyncio Task. Each task sees its own
82: # run_id + cost without threading explicit kwargs through the call graph.
83: # Synchronous / serial callers still work because ContextVar defaults
84: # to the module-default if no set_* has run in the current task.
85: import contextvars
86: 
87: _RUN_COST_LOCK = __import__("threading").Lock()
88: _RUN_COST_CTX: contextvars.ContextVar[float] = contextvars.ContextVar(
89:     "_RUN_COST_USD", default=0.0,
90: )
91: _CURRENT_RUN_ID_CTX: contextvars.ContextVar[str | None] = contextvars.ContextVar(
92:     "_CURRENT_RUN_ID", default=None,
93: )
94: 
95: 
96: def set_current_run_id(run_id: str | None) -> None:
97:     """Set the ambient run-id for the current async task. Each
98:     asyncio.Task has its own context, so concurrent runs don't stomp.
99:     """
100:     _CURRENT_RUN_ID_CTX.set(run_id)
101: 
102: 
103: def current_run_id() -> str | None:
104:     return _CURRENT_RUN_ID_CTX.get()
105: 
106: 
107: # I-gen-004 (#496): run-scoped reasoning-trace capture. The generator wires a
108: # ReasoningTraceCollector as the sink (set_reasoning_sink); the client records
109: # each raw completed provider response to it (_capture_reasoning_trace) BEFORE
110: # any caller-level promotion / </think> extraction / retry / truncation raise.
111: # Layering: the sink is duck-typed (the client never imports the generator).
112: # The per-call generator context (section, call_type, attempt_n, ...) is
113: # supplied separately by the caller via set_reasoning_call_context; capture is
114: # a no-op unless BOTH a sink and a call-context are present, which scopes it
115: # to generator calls (evaluator / retrieval LLM calls set no context).
116: _REASONING_SINK_CTX: contextvars.ContextVar[object | None] = contextvars.ContextVar(
117:     "_REASONING_SINK", default=None,
118: )
119: _REASONING_CALL_CTX: contextvars.ContextVar[dict | None] = contextvars.ContextVar(
120:     "_REASONING_CALL_CTX", default=None,
121: )
122: 
123: 
124: def set_reasoning_sink(sink: object | None) -> None:
125:     """Register (or clear, with None) the run-scoped reasoning-trace sink — a
126:     duck-typed object exposing ``record(**fields) -> call_id``. Set at run
127:     start, cleared at run end."""
128:     _REASONING_SINK_CTX.set(sink)
129: 
130: 
131: def current_reasoning_sink() -> object | None:
132:     return _REASONING_SINK_CTX.get()
133: 
134: 
135: def set_reasoning_call_context(**ctx: object) -> None:
136:     """Set the per-call generator context (section, call_type, attempt_n,
137:     parent_call_id, regen_reason) for the next captured provider response.
138:     Generator call sites set this immediately before each LLM call; passing
139:     no kwargs clears it (a non-generator call records nothing)."""
140:     _REASONING_CALL_CTX.set(dict(ctx) if ctx else None)
141: 
142: 
143: def current_reasoning_call_context() -> dict | None:
144:     return _REASONING_CALL_CTX.get()
145: 
146: 
147: def _capture_reasoning_trace(
148:     result: "LLMResponse",
149:     content: str,
150:     reasoning: Optional[str],
151:     *,
152:     status: str = "ok",
153: ) -> None:
154:     """Record one raw completed provider response to the run-scoped
155:     reasoning-trace sink, if one is registered AND the caller set a generator
156:     call-context. ``content_source`` is recorded as ``direct`` here;
157:     generate()/reason() finalize it (promoted_from_reasoning /
158:     extracted_from_reasoning) via the sink's update() using
159:     ``result.trace_call_id``. Best-effort observability — a sink failure is
160:     logged loud but never breaks the LLM call (reasoning trace is process
161:     transparency, not the generation path)."""
162:     sink = current_reasoning_sink()
163:     ctx = current_reasoning_call_context()
164:     if sink is None or not ctx:
165:         return
166:     try:
167:         result.trace_call_id = sink.record(
168:             section=str(ctx.get("section", "")),
169:             call_type=str(ctx.get("call_type", "section")),
170:             model=result.model or "",
171:             status=status,
172:             content_source="direct",
173:             parent_call_id=ctx.get("parent_call_id"),
174:             regen_reason=ctx.get("regen_reason"),
175:             attempt_n=int(ctx.get("attempt_n", 1) or 1),
176:             reasoning_text=reasoning or "",
177:             content_text=content or "",
178:             input_tokens=result.input_tokens,
179:             output_tokens=result.output_tokens,
180:             reasoning_tokens=result.reasoning_tokens,
181:         )
182:     except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — observability must not break generation
183:         logger.warning(
184:             "[polaris graph] I-gen-004: reasoning-trace capture failed "
185:             "(%s: %s) — generation continues, this record dropped",
186:             type(exc).__name__,
187:             exc,
188:         )
189: 
190: 
191: def _finalize_reasoning_trace(call_id: Optional[str], **patch: object) -> None:
192:     """I-gen-004 (#496): patch an already-captured reasoning-trace record once
193:     the caller (generate()/reason()) has resolved promotion / `</think>`
194:     extraction / retry / truncation — e.g. content_source=promoted_from_reasoning
195:     or status=retry|truncated|error. No-op if no sink or no call_id.
196:     Best-effort: a sink failure is logged, never raised."""
197:     if not call_id:
198:         return
199:     sink = current_reasoning_sink()
200:     if sink is None:
201:         return
202:     try:
203:         sink.update(call_id, **patch)
204:     except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — observability must not break generation
205:         logger.warning(
206:             "[polaris graph] I-gen-004: reasoning-trace finalize failed "
207:             "(%s: %s) — generation continues",
208:             type(exc).__name__,
209:             exc,
210:         )
211: 
212: 
213: class BudgetExceededError(RuntimeError):
214:     """Raised when PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN is breached mid-run."""
215: 
216: 
217: class ReasoningFirstTruncationError(RuntimeError):
218:     """I-bug-089 / I-gen-003: a reasoning-first model (DeepSeek V4 Pro/
219:     Flash) ran out of token budget while still planning — content is
220:     empty and reasoning_content has no provenance markers and ends
221:     mid-sentence. Typed (not bare RuntimeError) so the multi_section
222:     generator's _call_section can catch *only* this case and let the
223:     bounded regen loop retry with a larger budget, while every other
224:     RuntimeError still propagates as a real fault."""
225: 
226: 
227: def reset_run_cost() -> None:
228:     """Reset the current task's run-cost accumulator to 0. Call at the
229:     start of a run. Uses ContextVar so concurrent async tasks each get
230:     their own zero-start."""
231:     _RUN_COST_CTX.set(0.0)
232: 
233: 
234: def current_run_cost() -> float:
235:     """Return the cumulative cost of the current run (USD), scoped to
236:     the current asyncio task via ContextVar."""
237:     return _RUN_COST_CTX.get()
238: 
239: 
240: def _add_run_cost(delta: float) -> None:
241:     # BUG-B-201: ContextVar-scoped cost accumulator. Each async task has
242:     # its own run-cost; concurrent gather() of run_one_query() no longer
243:     # stomps each other.
244:     _RUN_COST_CTX.set(_RUN_COST_CTX.get() + float(delta))
245: 
246: 
247: # Codex round 1 B-4: conservative per-model prices used when OpenRouter
248: # omits usage.cost. Prices are $/M tokens and represent the UPPER END of
249: # published rates for each provider family (if a cheaper provider is used
250: # we overcharge the run cost, which is the safe direction for a budget
251: # guard). When the model is unknown we use OPUS-tier defaults.
252: _PRICE_TABLE_USD_PER_M: dict[str, tuple[float, float]] = {
253:     # model prefix  :  (input $/M, output $/M)
254:     # IMPORTANT: longer/more-specific prefixes first. Dict insertion order
255:     # = iteration order = first-match-wins per _impute_cost_from_tokens.
256:     # I-cd-010 / GH#625: qwen/qwen3-8b + z-ai/glm-5.1 entries are
257:     # INTENTIONAL pricing-table coverage for env-overridden models (set
258:     # PG_GENERATOR_MODEL / OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL); not the active
259:     # Carney demo lock defaults.
260:     "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro":   (0.435, 0.87),
261:     "deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash": (0.14, 0.28),
262:     "deepseek/":       (0.27, 0.38),
263:     "qwen/qwen3-8b":   (0.05, 0.40),
264:     "qwen/qwen3-32b":  (0.10, 0.60),
265:     "qwen/":           (0.10, 0.60),
266:     "z-ai/glm-5.1":    (0.60, 2.20),
267:     "z-ai/":           (0.60, 2.20),
268:     "meta-llama/":     (0.30, 0.90),
269:     "google/gemma-4-31b-it": (0.13, 0.38),
270:     "google/gemma":    (0.05, 0.30),
271:     "google/gemini":   (1.25, 5.00),
272:     "mistralai/":      (0.30, 0.90),
273:     "moonshotai/":     (0.60, 2.50),
274:     "openai/":         (3.00, 10.00),
275:     "anthropic/":      (3.00, 15.00),
276: }
277: 
278: _DEFAULT_PRICE_PER_M = (3.00, 15.00)  # Opus-tier worst-case
279: 
280: 
281: def _impute_cost_from_tokens(
282:     model: str,
283:     input_tokens: int,
284:     output_tokens: int,
285:     reasoning_tokens: int,
286: ) -> float:
287:     """Estimate $/call from token counts + published prices. Reasoning
288:     tokens bill at the output rate (matches OpenAI / Anthropic practice).
289:     Used by the budget guard when OpenRouter omits usage.cost.
290: 
291:     Defensive: token counts are clamped to >=0. A corrupted API response
292:     returning negative tokens must NOT produce a negative cost that
293:     would silently shrink the accumulated run budget and let a runaway
294:     loop keep calling past PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN.
295:     """
296:     # Clamp negative token counts — these can only come from a
297:     # corrupted API response. A negative cost here would be silently
298:     # absorbed by _add_run_cost and weaken the budget guard.
299:     input_tokens = max(0, int(input_tokens))
300:     output_tokens = max(0, int(output_tokens))
301:     reasoning_tokens = max(0, int(reasoning_tokens))
302:     if input_tokens == 0 and output_tokens == 0 and reasoning_tokens == 0:
303:         return 0.0
304:     model_lower = (model or "").lower()
305:     input_rate, output_rate = _DEFAULT_PRICE_PER_M
306:     for prefix, (in_r, out_r) in _PRICE_TABLE_USD_PER_M.items():
307:         if model_lower.startswith(prefix):
308:             input_rate, output_rate = in_r, out_r
309:             break
310:     return (
311:         (input_tokens / 1_000_000.0) * input_rate
312:         + ((output_tokens + reasoning_tokens) / 1_000_000.0) * output_rate
313:     )
314: 
315: 
316: def check_run_budget(anticipated_additional: float = 0.0) -> None:
317:     """Raise BudgetExceededError if the next call would exceed the cap.
318: 
319:     anticipated_additional: optional estimate of the upcoming call cost
320:     (used only for guard-before-call — the actual cost is recorded by
321:     `_add_run_cost()` after the call returns).
322:     """
323:     current = current_run_cost()
324:     projected = current + max(0.0, float(anticipated_additional))
325:     if projected > PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN:
326:         raise BudgetExceededError(
327:             f"PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN={PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN:.4f} exceeded: "
328:             f"current=${current:.4f} + anticipated=${anticipated_additional:.4f} "
329:             f"= ${projected:.4f}. Set PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN higher or call "
330:             f"reset_run_cost() between runs."
331:         )
332:

336: # training lineages to avoid self-bias (Play Favorites arXiv:2508.06709 Aug
337: # 2025; DeepHalluBench arXiv:2601.22984 Jan 2026).
338: #
339: # Defaults pin the VERIFIED-ON-GROUNDED-FACTUALITY pair (user decision

359: #         token; preferred for narrow judgment tasks where consistency
360: #         matters more than raw capability)

256: def test_judge_raises_budget_exceeded_when_cap_breached(monkeypatch, tmp_path):

1701: 
1702:         # SF-30: Token usage from response — warn if missing
1703:         usage_data = data.get("usage", {})
1704:         if not usage_data:
1705:             # FIX-O2: Estimate tokens from content length instead of defaulting to 0
1706:             est_input = sum(len(m.get("content", "")) for m in messages) // 4
1707:             est_output = len(content) // 4 if content else 0
1708:             logger.warning(
1709:                 "[polaris graph] FIX-O2: API response missing usage data for %s "
1710:                 "— estimating %d/%d tokens from content length",
1711:                 call_type, est_input, est_output,
1712:             )
1713:             usage_data = {
1714:                 "prompt_tokens": est_input,
1715:                 "completion_tokens": est_output,
1716:             }
1717:         input_tokens = usage_data.get("prompt_tokens", 0)
1718:         output_tokens = usage_data.get("completion_tokens", 0)
1719:         reasoning_tokens = usage_data.get("reasoning_tokens", 0)
1720:         # FIX-QM11b: OpenRouter nests reasoning_tokens inside
1721:         # completion_tokens_details, not at top level of usage
1722:         if not reasoning_tokens:
1723:             ctd = usage_data.get("completion_tokens_details", {})
1724:             if ctd:
1725:                 reasoning_tokens = ctd.get("reasoning_tokens", 0)
1726: 
1727:         # FIX-C2: Extract API-reported cost for accurate billing.
1728:         # Codex round 1 B-4: OpenRouter omits `usage.cost` for some models,
1729:         # which let the run budget record $0.00 even when tokens were
1730:         # consumed. Now: if cost is missing AND tokens were consumed,
1731:         # impute a conservative upper bound from published per-model rates.
1732:         api_cost = usage_data.get("cost", None)
1733:         if api_cost is None or api_cost == 0:
1734:             # Use per-model price table; defaults reflect observed upper-
1735:             # bound ($10/M output for opus-tier models) so the guard is
1736:             # always conservative. Token totals may be 0 if the response
1737:             # carried no usage block at all.
1738:             imputed = _impute_cost_from_tokens(
1739:                 self.model, input_tokens, output_tokens, reasoning_tokens,
1740:             )
1741:             if api_cost is None and imputed > 0:
1742:                 logger.warning(
1743:                     "[polaris graph] B-4: OpenRouter omitted usage.cost "
1744:                     "for model=%r; imputed cost=$%.6f from tokens "
1745:                     "(in=%d out=%d reasoning=%d). Budget guard uses the "
1746:                     "imputed value so a missing cost field cannot bypass "
1747:                     "PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN.",
1748:                     self.model, imputed, input_tokens, output_tokens,
1749:                     reasoning_tokens,
1750:                 )
1751:             api_cost = max(api_cost or 0.0, imputed)
1752: 
1753:         # Track usage
1754:         self.usage.record(
1755:             call_type=call_type,
1756:             input_tokens=input_tokens,
1757:             output_tokens=output_tokens,
1758:             reasoning_tokens=reasoning_tokens,
1759:             duration_ms=duration_ms,
1760:             api_cost=api_cost,
1761:         )
1762:         # R-2: contribute to the shared run-cost counter AFTER the call.
1763:         # Checking the cap BEFORE is done by callers via check_run_budget().
1764:         _add_run_cost(api_cost)
1765: 
1766:         # COT-1: Strict separation — NEVER mix reasoning into content.
1767:         # The API separates content and reasoning_content correctly.
1768:         # Callers (generate(), reason(), generate_structured()) handle
1769:         # empty content at their own level with retry + extraction.
1770:         # FIX-H2: Explicit warning and raise when BOTH content and reasoning are empty.
1771:         # This catches degenerate API responses (0-token completions) instead of
1772:         # silently returning empty strings that cascade into downstream failures.
1773:         if not content and not reasoning:
1774:             logger.warning(
1775:                 "[polaris graph] FIX-H2: LLM returned empty content AND empty "
1776:                 "reasoning_content for %s (model=%s, %d input tokens)",
1777:                 call_type,
1778:                 data.get("model", self.model),
1779:                 input_tokens,
1780:             )
1781:             raise ValueError(
1782:                 f"LLM returned no usable content for {call_type} "
1783:                 f"(model={data.get('model', '?')}, input_tokens={input_tokens})"
1784:             )
1785: 
1786:         if not content and reasoning:
1787:             logger.warning(
1788:                 "[polaris graph] COT-1: Content empty for %s, reasoning has "
1789:                 "%d chars (reasoning_enabled=%s, response_format=%s). "
1790:                 "Returning as-is — caller handles recovery.",
1791:                 call_type,
1792:                 len(reasoning),
1793:                 reasoning_enabled,
1794:                 bool(response_format),
1795:             )
1796: 
1797:         result = LLMResponse(
1798:             content=content,
1799:             reasoning=reasoning,
1800:             input_tokens=input_tokens,
1801:             output_tokens=output_tokens,
1802:             reasoning_tokens=reasoning_tokens,
1803:             model=data.get("model", self.model),
1804:             duration_ms=duration_ms,
1805:             raw_response=data,
1806:         )
1807: 
1808:         # I-gen-004 (#496): capture the raw completed provider response to the
1809:         # run-scoped reasoning-trace sink BEFORE any caller-level promotion /
1810:         # </think> extraction / retry / ReasoningFirstTruncationError raise.
1811:         _capture_reasoning_trace(result, content, reasoning)
1812: 
1813:         # I-safety-002b (#925): Path-B benchmark gate capture — one LLMCall per provider
1814:         # completion (this is the unified completion boundary for stream + non-stream +
1815:         # retries + reason/generate/generate_structured). Best-effort + gate-flagged:
1816:         # is_active() is a cheap contextvar read and is False outside a benchmark run.
1817:         # PR-2: capture ONLY explicitly-tagged calls (Codex APPROVE Option B). Auxiliary
1818:         # calls (scope LLM, inductor) without a role tag are NOT captured / NOT gated —
1819:         # the gate polices the REPORT generator + REPORT evaluators only, which is what
1820:         # the benchmark needs and is robust to auxiliary-model config changes.
1821:         if _pathb_capture.is_active():
1822:             _role = _pathb_capture.current_llm_role()
1823:             if _role is not None:
1824:                 _pathb_capture.capture_llm_call(
1825:                     role=_role,
1826:                     messages=sanitized_messages,
1827:                     raw_response=data,
1828:                 )
1829: 
1830:         logger.info(
1831:             "[polaris graph] %s completed: %d in/%d out/%d reasoning tokens, "
1832:             "%.1fs, $%.4f cumulative",
1833:             call_type,
1834:             input_tokens,
1835:             output_tokens,
1836:             reasoning_tokens,
1837:             duration_ms / 1000,
1838:             self.usage.total_cost_usd,
1839:         )
1840: 
1841:         # OBS-COST: Emit authoritative llm_call trace with real tokens + cost.
1842:         # This is the SINGLE source of truth — agents no longer need to pass
1843:         # tokens to tracer.llm_call() since the client does it here.
1844:         _cost_tracer = _current_tracer.get(None)
1845:         if _cost_tracer is not None:
1846:             _node = call_type.split(":")[0] if ":" in call_type else "llm"
1847:             _cost_tracer.llm_call(
1848:                 node=_node,
1849:                 call_type=call_type,
1850:                 input_tokens=input_tokens,
1851:                 output_tokens=output_tokens,
1852:                 duration_ms=duration_ms,
1853:                 reasoning_tokens=reasoning_tokens,
1854:                 cost_usd=round(
1855:                     api_cost if api_cost > 0 else
1856:                     (input_tokens / 1_000_000) * INPUT_COST_PER_M
1857:                     + (output_tokens / 1_000_000) * OUTPUT_COST_PER_M,
1858:                     6,
1859:                 ),
1860:                 cumulative_cost_usd=round(self.usage.total_cost_usd, 4),
1861:                 model=self.model[:50],
1862:             )
1863: 
1864:         # OBS-REASONING: Persist reasoning to trace JSONL
1865:         if reasoning and isinstance(reasoning, str) and len(reasoning) > 10:
1866:             _obs_tracer = _current_tracer.get(None)
1867:             if _obs_tracer is not None:
1868:                 _obs_tracer.reasoning_capture(
1869:                     node=call_type.split(":")[0] if ":" in call_type else "llm",
1870:                     call_type=call_type,
1871:                     reasoning_text=reasoning,
1872:                     prompt_excerpt=(messages[-1].get("content") or "")[:1000] if messages else "",
1873:                     input_tokens=input_tokens,
1874:                     output_tokens=output_tokens,
1875:                     reasoning_tokens=reasoning_tokens,
1876:                 )
1877: 
1878:         # WAVE-1.3: Full LLM prompt + response trace (100% visibility)
1879:         # Every _call() emits complete prompt messages, response, reasoning,
1880:         # and all parameters. ~80-120 events per run, ~2-5MB total.
1881:         _detail_tracer = _current_tracer.get(None)
1882:         if _detail_tracer is not None:
1883:             _detail_node = call_type.split(":")[0] if ":" in call_type else "llm"
1884:             _detail_cost = round(
1885:                 api_cost if api_cost > 0 else
1886:                 (input_tokens / 1_000_000) * INPUT_COST_PER_M
1887:                 + (output_tokens / 1_000_000) * OUTPUT_COST_PER_M,
1888:                 6,
1889:             )
1890:             _detail_tracer._emit("llm_detail", _detail_node, {

1892:                 "model": self.model,
1893:                 "temperature": body.get("temperature"),
1894:                 "max_tokens": body.get("max_tokens"),

1896:                 "response_format": str(body.get("response_format", "")),
1897:                 "prompt_messages": [
1898:                     {"role": m.get("role", ""), "content": m.get("content", "")}
1899:                     for m in messages

1907:                 "cost_usd": _detail_cost,
1908:             })
1909: 
1910:         return result

1: """Entailment judge — LLM-as-judge for strict_verify check (f).
2: 
3: Originally inlined in `polaris_graph.clinical_generator.strict_verify`. Extracted
4: per I-bug-099 so future call sites (I-bug-100 OpenRouterClient routing,
5: I-bug-101 FPR audit harness, etc.) can import the judge + telemetry from
6: a single canonical location.
7: 
8: I-bug-102 — off-mode contract (verified by `test_off_mode_does_not_instantiate_judge`):
9: when `PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=off`, the judge is NEVER instantiated:
10: - `_get_judge()` is not called from any code path
11: - `_EntailmentJudge.__init__` does NOT run
12: - No `httpx.Client` is constructed
13: - No OpenRouter network call is made
14: The class definitions are loaded into the module namespace at import
15: time (Python module evaluation), but no judge object exists. Module-
16: level imports are kept lightweight: `openrouter_client` is imported
17: lazily inside the methods that need it (judge construction, ledger
18: write, budget check), so off-mode pays zero cost beyond reading the
19: class definitions.
20: 
21: The judge asks an LLM whether a cited SPAN semantically ENTAILS a
22: SENTENCE's specific claims. It runs AFTER mechanical checks (a)-(e) and
23: catches residual fabrication patterns the audit on 2026-05-09 surfaced:
24:   - mechanistic granularity insertion (M2)
25:   - specificity inflation (C2)
26:   - numbers nearby but not entailed (C1)
27: 
28: The judge model is the two-family evaluator (Gemma 4 31B by default),
29: matching the §9.1.1 invariant — different lineage from the generator
30: (DeepSeek). Calls go through OpenRouter using the existing project
31: auth substrate. Family segregation is enforced at construction.
32: 
33: Public surface (re-exported via `polaris_graph.clinical_generator.strict_verify`
34: for backwards compat with monkeypatch test pattern):
35:   - _EntailmentJudge: synchronous httpx wrapper around an OpenRouter call
36:   - _get_judge(): lazy singleton accessor
37:   - _JUDGE_TELEMETRY: process-lifetime gate counters
38:   - get_judge_telemetry(): read-only snapshot
39:   - reset_judge_telemetry(): zero in-place
40:   - _record_judge_outcome(): tick counters from judge result
41: 
42: NOT moved here (still in strict_verify.py because tests rebind via
43: monkeypatch.setattr on strict_verify):
44:   - _DEFAULT_MODE
45:   - _UNKNOWN_MODE_WARNED
46:   - _entailment_mode()
47: """

51: import json
52: import logging
53: import os
54: import time
55: from datetime import datetime, timezone
56: 
57: # I-bug-100: import openrouter_client as a MODULE reference (not by-value)
58: # so monkeypatch.setattr on its globals (`PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN`,
59: # `_COST_LEDGER_PATH`, etc.) propagates to the cost-accounting code paths
60: # in this module. Direct `from … import _COST_LEDGER_PATH` would bind
61: # the value at import time and tests could not override it without
62: # reloading the module.
63: #
64: # I-bug-102 NOTE: this top-level import IS evaluated even in off-mode.
65: # The "skip clinical_generator import" goal in I-bug-102 is interpreted as
66: # "no JUDGE INSTANTIATION + no NETWORK CALL in off-mode" (verified by
67: # `test_off_mode_does_not_instantiate_judge`), NOT as eliminating the
68: # module-import side effect. Empirically the openrouter_client import
69: # is ~50ms cold; off-mode users pay this once at strict_verify import
70: # time. Eliminating it via per-method lazy-import would complicate the
71: # `except _orc.BudgetExceededError` semantics (exception types must be
72: # resolved at except-clause evaluation, which is a hot-path call).
73: # The test asserts the runtime contract; cold-import cost is accepted.

80: _ENTAILMENT_TIMEOUT_S = 30.0
81: _ENTAILMENT_PROMPT = """You are a strict entailment judge. You will be given a SPAN of source text and a SENTENCE that cites that span. Decide whether the SPAN entails the SENTENCE.
82: 
83: Rules:
84: - ENTAILED: every factual assertion in the SENTENCE is supported by the SPAN. Conservative paraphrase is allowed.
85: - NEUTRAL: the SENTENCE introduces a fact, entity, mechanism, or specificity NOT present in the SPAN (e.g. SPAN says "GLP-1 RAs", SENTENCE says "semaglutide"; SPAN says "adipocyte metabolism", SENTENCE adds "lipid metabolism" or "energy storage"; SPAN has numbers but not the specific claim being made).
86: - CONTRADICTED: the SENTENCE asserts something the SPAN explicitly disagrees with.
87: 
88: Return STRICT JSON only, no prose:
89: {{"verdict": "ENTAILED" | "NEUTRAL" | "CONTRADICTED", "reason": "<one short sentence>"}}
90: 
91: SPAN:
92: {span}
93: 
94: SENTENCE:
95: {sentence}
96: 
97: JSON:"""

100: class _EntailmentJudge:
101:     """Synchronous httpx wrapper around an OpenRouter entailment call.
102: 
103:     Lazy-initialized via _get_judge() so import-time cost is zero when
104:     PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=off (the default before I-bug-095).
105:     """
106: 
107:     def __init__(self) -> None:
108:         import httpx  # local import: avoid forcing the dep when off
109: 
110:         # Lazy-import the family-segregation check so the off-mode path
111:         # never touches openrouter_client. The judge is acting as a
112:         # content evaluator (Layer-2), so it MUST differ from the
113:         # generator family per CLAUDE.md §9.1.1 — fail at construction
114:         # if PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL is in the same family as PG_GENERATOR_MODEL.
115:         from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import check_family_segregation
116: 
117:         api_key = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "").strip()
118:         if not api_key:
119:             raise RuntimeError(
120:                 "PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT requires OPENROUTER_API_KEY"
121:             )
122:         self._api_key = api_key
123:         # I-sov-001: endpoint is env-configurable so the sovereign deploy
124:         # can point the entailment judge at the OVH H200 vLLM endpoint
125:         # (set OPENROUTER_BASE_URL=http://<priv-ip>:8000/v1). Default keeps
126:         # OpenRouter. Mirrors openrouter_client.py:43-45.
127:         base_url = os.environ.get(
128:             "OPENROUTER_BASE_URL", "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"
129:         ).rstrip("/")
130:         self._endpoint = f"{base_url}/chat/completions"
131:         self._model = os.environ.get(
132:             "PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL", _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL
133:         )
134:         # Two-family invariant per §9.1.1: raises RuntimeError if the
135:         # entailment judge ends up in the same family as the generator
136:         # (e.g. an operator setting PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL to a DeepSeek
137:         # variant when PG_GENERATOR_MODEL is also DeepSeek). The
138:         # default model (google/gemma-4-31b-it) is in a different
139:         # family from DeepSeek by construction.
140:         check_family_segregation(evaluator_model=self._model)
141:         self._client = httpx.Client(timeout=_ENTAILMENT_TIMEOUT_S)
142: 
143:     def judge(self, sentence: str, span: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
144:         """Return (verdict, reason).
145: 
146:         verdict is one of "ENTAILED", "NEUTRAL", "CONTRADICTED".
147:         On API/parse failure returns ("ENTAILED", "judge_error: ...") —
148:         fail-open so a transient OpenRouter outage does not nuke a run.
149: 
150:         I-bug-100: after each successful httpx call this method records
151:         the call cost via openrouter_client's module-level helpers
152:         (`_add_run_cost`, `check_run_budget`, `_COST_LEDGER_PATH`) so
153:         judge spend is visible to the per-run budget cap and the cost
154:         ledger. `BudgetExceededError` is explicitly re-raised before
155:         the broad `except Exception` fail-open handler so a cap breach
156:         aborts the sweep cleanly instead of being masked as a transient
157:         judge error.
158:         """
159:         prompt = _ENTAILMENT_PROMPT.format(span=span, sentence=sentence)
160:         started = time.monotonic()
161:         # I-bug-946 (#932): when Path-B gate is active, force singleton provider routing in
162:         # the request body to match the resolved-at-preflight per-role provider. Without this,
163:         # this direct httpx path bypasses the gate's routing intent (the OpenRouterClient path
164:         # also got the override via openrouter_client.py:1400-1410). Codex iter-2 P1#2.
165:         # Codex iter-1 diff P1#2: this lookup MUST use explicit role="evaluator", NOT the
166:         # ambient _ROLE contextvar. The entailment judge fires during section generation
167:         # (where _ROLE=="generator"), but it posts the evaluator-family model — using the
168:         # ambient role would route Gemma to the generator's provider (Fireworks, no Gemma).
169:         try:
170:             from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture as _pathb_for_routing
171:             _gate_provider = _pathb_for_routing.get_role_provider("evaluator")
172:         except Exception:
173:             _gate_provider = None
174:         json_body: dict = {
175:             "model": self._model,
176:             "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
177:             "temperature": 0.0,
178:             "max_tokens": 100,
179:             "response_format": {"type": "json_object"},
180:         }
181:         if _gate_provider:
182:             json_body["provider"] = {
183:                 "order": [_gate_provider],
184:                 "allow_fallbacks": False,
185:                 "require_parameters": True,
186:             }
187:         try:
188:             response = self._client.post(
189:                 self._endpoint,
190:                 headers={
191:                     "Authorization": f"Bearer {self._api_key}",
192:                     "Content-Type": "application/json",
193:                 },
194:                 json=json_body,
195:             )
196:             response.raise_for_status()
197:             data = response.json()
198: 
199:             # I-safety-002b (#925): Path-B gate capture. The entailment judge is the
200:             # evaluator-family LLM call that bypasses OpenRouterClient (direct httpx),
201:             # so without capturing here the gate's two-family completeness check would
202:             # be a silent no-op. Best-effort + gate-flagged; lazy import keeps off-mode
203:             # import cost zero. `data` is the genuinely-served non-stream JSON.
204:             try:
205:                 from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture as _pathb
206:                 if _pathb.is_active():
207:                     _pathb.capture_llm_call(
208:                         role="evaluator",
209:                         messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
210:                         raw_response=data,
211:                     )
212:             except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — capture must never break the judge
213:                 pass
214: 
215:             # I-bug-100: cost recording. Reads + records BEFORE verdict
216:             # parse so a cap breach aborts the sweep regardless of
217:             # downstream parse outcome.
218:             usage = data.get("usage", {}) or {}
219:             input_tokens = int(usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0) or 0)
220:             output_tokens = int(usage.get("completion_tokens", 0) or 0)
221:             api_cost = float(usage.get("cost", 0) or 0)
222:             actual_cost = api_cost or _orc._impute_cost_from_tokens(
223:                 self._model, input_tokens, output_tokens, 0,
224:             )
225:             # I-bug-100 iter-1 diff P2 fix: when the entire usage block
226:             # is absent, both api_cost and the imputed value are 0, which
227:             # silently bypasses the budget guard. Fall back to a
228:             # conservative estimate based on typical judge-call shape
229:             # (~500 prompt + ~100 completion tokens) priced at Opus-tier
230:             # so the budget cap is preserved on degraded responses.
231:             if actual_cost == 0 and not usage:
232:                 actual_cost = _orc._impute_cost_from_tokens(
233:                     self._model, 500, 100, 0,
234:                 )
235:             _orc._add_run_cost(actual_cost)
236:             duration_ms = (time.monotonic() - started) * 1000.0
237:             try:
238:                 _append_judge_ledger_entry(
239:                     input_tokens=input_tokens,
240:                     output_tokens=output_tokens,
241:                     duration_ms=duration_ms,
242:                     actual_cost=actual_cost,
243:                 )
244:             except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — ledger IO is non-critical
245:                 logger.warning("entailment ledger write failed: %s", exc)
246:             _orc.check_run_budget(0)  # raises BudgetExceededError if cap breached
247: 
248:             content = data["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]
249:             parsed = json.loads(content)
250:             verdict = str(parsed.get("verdict", "")).upper().strip()
251:             reason = str(parsed.get("reason", ""))
252:             if verdict not in ("ENTAILED", "NEUTRAL", "CONTRADICTED"):
253:                 return "ENTAILED", f"judge_error: bad_verdict={verdict!r}"
254:             return verdict, reason
255:         except _orc.BudgetExceededError:
256:             # I-bug-100: do NOT fail-open on cap breach. Propagate so
257:             # the sweep aborts with a clear cause.
258:             raise
259:         except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open by design
260:             logger.warning("entailment judge error: %s", exc)
261:             return "ENTAILED", f"judge_error: {type(exc).__name__}"
262: 
263: 
264: def _append_judge_ledger_entry(
265:     *,
266:     input_tokens: int,
267:     output_tokens: int,
268:     duration_ms: float,
269:     actual_cost: float,
270: ) -> None:
271:     """Append a single entailment-judge call to the cost ledger.
272: 
273:     Schema mirrors `openrouter_client.OpenRouterClient._append_ledger`
274:     (line ~481-491) so per-run filters that key on `session_id` /
275:     `call_type` (e.g., scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py) include judge
276:     calls without code changes downstream.
277: 
278:     All `_orc.<attr>` accesses go through the module reference so
279:     test monkeypatch.setattr on `openrouter_client._COST_LEDGER_PATH`
280:     propagates correctly.
281:     """
282:     entry = {
283:         "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
284:         "session_id": _orc._CURRENT_RUN_ID_CTX.get() or "no_run_id",
285:         "call_type": "entailment_judge",
286:         "input_tokens": input_tokens,
287:         "output_tokens": output_tokens,
288:         "reasoning_tokens": 0,
289:         "duration_ms": round(duration_ms, 1),
290:         "cost_usd": round(actual_cost, 6),
291:         "cumulative_cost_usd": round(_orc.current_run_cost(), 4),
292:     }
293:     _orc._COST_LEDGER_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
294:     with open(_orc._COST_LEDGER_PATH, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
295:         f.write(json.dumps(entry) + "\n")
296: 
297: 
298: _JUDGE_SINGLETON: _EntailmentJudge | None = None
299: 
300: 
301: def _get_judge() -> _EntailmentJudge:
302:     """Lazy singleton so off-mode pays zero import/connection cost."""
303:     global _JUDGE_SINGLETON
304:     if _JUDGE_SINGLETON is None:
305:         _JUDGE_SINGLETON = _EntailmentJudge()
306:     return _JUDGE_SINGLETON
307: 
308: 
309: # I-bug-096: process-lifetime telemetry counters for the entailment gate.
310: # In enforce mode the judge fail-open path returns ("ENTAILED",
311: # "judge_error: ...") on transient API errors. A persistent OpenRouter
312: # outage or model-format change could make the 6th check silently inert
313: # — every sentence falls through as ENTAILED while WARNING lines
314: # accumulate. These counters give an operator a concise "the gate ran N
315: # times, M of those errored" signal.
316: #
317: # Counters are gate-side (not judge-side) per the original I-bug-092
318: # brief verdict: gate behavior is what we want to measure, not a
319: # specific judge implementation. Tests with FakeJudge tick these too,
320: # and a future swapped judge cannot bypass the judge_error counter.
321: _JUDGE_TELEMETRY: dict[str, int] = {
322:     "calls": 0,
323:     "entailed": 0,
324:     "neutral": 0,
325:     "contradicted": 0,
326:     "judge_error": 0,
327: }
328: 
329: 
330: def get_judge_telemetry() -> dict[str, int]:
