docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:135:### 3.3 Qwen3-235B-A22B
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:137:**Pros.** Apache 2.0 — cleanest commercial license. Active 22B param footprint → cheaper inference than V4 Pro at comparable scale. SuperGPQA family-leader (Qwen3.5-397B at 0.704; Qwen3.6+ at 0.716). MMLU-ProX leader (Qwen3.5-397B at 84.7, Qwen3.6 Plus at 84.7). 100+ languages including strong FR. FinanceBench Capability leader (73.0 via Qwen3-32B sibling). AA-Omniscience: Qwen3.7 Max hallucination rate 22.9% (lowest in frontier group, down from 44.2% on Qwen3.6).
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:139:**Cons.** Vectara HHEM-2.3 hallucination rate is **9.3% with 94.9% answer rate** — UNDER-abstains less than Qwen3-8B (4.8%) and OVER-hallucinates more. The Qwen family trades accuracy for raw fluency. HLE = 11.7 (weakest reasoning of the frontier candidates). Long-context behavior beyond 128K is UNKNOWN; YaRN scaling to 131K but no RULER scores per-length located. License governance: Alibaba started releasing some 2026 Qwen models (3.6-Plus, 3.5-Omni) as proprietary, but the 235B-A22B and the 3.6-35B-A3B remain Apache 2.0 per the official Qwen blog [^q3_blog].
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:145:### 3.4 Qwen3-32B / 14B / 8B (dense)
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:149:**Pros.** Apache 2.0. **Best Vectara hallucination rates** of the candidates (8B = 4.8%, 14B = 5.4%, 32B = 5.9% — lower than DeepSeek V3.2-Exp at 5.3%; 8B is the LOWEST of the entire open-weight non-US set). Strong answer rates (99.9%). Dense → easy to deploy at single-H100 (32B INT4) or single-T4 (8B). GPQA Diamond: 32B = 62.1, 14B = 59.1, 8B = 62.0 (size-saturated by ~8B). 32B is BFCL v3 strong (75.7).
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:155:**Community sentiment.** Qwen3-8B is the de facto "low-hallucination generator" in 2026 production RAG setups. Cited extensively in clinical-RAG SOTA literature (e.g., POLARIS's own `docs/clinical_rag_sota_deepest_research_2026_05_27.md`).
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:165:**Community sentiment.** Treated as the strongest EU-sovereign Apache-2.0 frontier model. The hallucination numbers are exceptional. Reasoning gap vs V4 Pro / Qwen3-235B is acknowledged.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:169:**Pros.** **Best IFEval score in the candidate set (92.1)** — strongest instruction-follower. Vectara HHEM-2.3 = 4.1% (best dense-model number). 99.5% answer rate. Mature inference everywhere. Family C (Meta) gives strongest two-family diversity vs DeepSeek + Qwen.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:181:**Cons.** Vectara HHEM-2.3 = **9.5% halluc rate, 94.5% answer rate** — second-worst of the candidates (only Qwen3-235B-A22B at 9.3% is comparable). MMLU-Pro UNKNOWN for 4.6 explicitly (4.5 = 84.6). Less stable maintenance cadence than DeepSeek / Qwen.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:187:### 3.8 Qwen2.5-Aloe-Beta-72B (Barcelona Supercomputing Center, healthcare-tuned)
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:189:**Pros.** **State-of-the-art medical performance with RAG** — outperforms MedPalm-2 and GPT-4 on MedQA / PubMedQA / MMLU-Medical / MedMCQA / CareQA per the [official HF model card](https://huggingface.co/HPAI-BSC/Qwen2.5-Aloe-Beta-72B). EU sovereignty (Barcelona Supercomputing Center). Inherits Qwen2.5 strong multilingual including FR. Open research community trusted; CSV-comparable evaluation method.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:193:**Latest issues.** [Aloe family paper (arxiv 2505.04388)](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2505.04388) documents the recipe. Updated 2025; no significant 2026 changes.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:201:**Osiris-7B (Qwen2.5 fine-tune)**: +22.8% recall over GPT-4o on RAGTruth. Three-way NLI native. Distributed via [judgmentlabs HF organisation](https://github.com/JudgmentLabs/osiris-detection). License: NOT explicitly stated in search results — needs direct HF card fetch. Qwen2.5 base is permissive (Qwen custom license); the Osiris fine-tune license is the load-bearing unknown.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:207:**Qwen3-32B / Llama 3.3 70B / GLM-4.6 as LLM-judge**: large LLMs used as judges via prompt engineering. Strengths inherit from the generator scores above. Weakness: judge-as-LLM still emits free-form CoT; Codex's revised schema (label + score + evidence_span + contradiction_span + abstain_reason) is enforceable via grammar but per-sentence latency is slow vs classifier verifiers.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:215:The single most striking pattern across the data: **hallucination and reasoning are NEGATIVELY correlated within DeepSeek's open-weight family**. V4 Pro is the strongest reasoner (37.7 HLE; 90.1 GPQA) BUT has the highest Vectara hallucination rate (8.6%). V3.2-Exp loses ~15 HLE points but cuts hallucination by ~40% (5.3%). Qwen3-8B has the LOWEST hallucination (4.8%) but cannot reason at frontier level.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:221:Apache 2.0 (Qwen3, Mistral Large 3) and MIT (DeepSeek V4 Pro, V3.2-Exp, GLM-4.6) are tied for cleanest commercial usability. Llama Community (Llama 3.3 70B) is the weakest license among capable candidates — the 700M MAU clause is forward-looking risk but the "ban on training other models" clause is immediate risk for any ensemble distillation. CC-BY-NC-4.0 (Aloe Beta 72B, Patronus Lynx) is commercially DISQUALIFIED.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:238:- RULER 128K per-model (only DeepSeek-V3 paper reports it; Qwen3 reports it differently; Llama 3.3 isn't on the NoLiMa leaderboard)

docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:246:The clear pattern: **classifier verifiers (HHEM-2.1-Open, VerifAI DeBERTa) win on speed + structured output reliability**; LLM-judges (Qwen3-32B, Llama 3.3 70B, GLM-4.6) win on reasoning depth + explanation quality but at 10-100x the latency cost. Patronus Lynx is the published-best LLM-judge but is commercially DISQUALIFIED.

docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:301:Layer 1  : DeepSeek V4 Pro generator + (optional) Qwen3-8B + Mistral Large 3 mirrors
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:309:Cross-family invariant: V4 Pro (DeepSeek) is family A. Gemma 4 31B (Google open) is family B. Qwen3-8B (Alibaba) is family C. Mistral Large 3 (Mistral EU) is family D. HHEM-2.1-Open (Vectara/T5/Google open) is family E. VerifAI DeBERTa (Microsoft DeBERTa) is family F. Six distinct training lineages → strong CHECK-style ensemble diversity.
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:319:1. **Citation existence + source attribution honesty (params 14-15)** — the dimension that matters MOST for Carney. ScholarQABench / CiteME / GhostCite all exist; the candidate set does not appear on them. **In-house: fetch 100 generated reports from V4 Pro + Qwen3-8B + Mistral Large 3, sample 10 claims per report, validate DOIs via OpenAlex API, validate paper title exact-match via PubMed/arXiv, validate cited span supports claim via human review.**
docs\polaris_model_selection_multi_param_2026_05_27.md:325:4. **RAG prompt-injection / AgentDojo (param 11)** — POLARIS feeds external documents into the generator. Prompt injection robustness is mission-critical. AgentDojo has 97 user tasks + 629 security cases. **In-house: run AgentDojo against V4 Pro + Qwen3-8B + Mistral Large 3 + Gemma 4 31B (the layered stack as a whole).**

docs\clinical_rag_sota_deepest_research_2026_05_27.md:28:**The single most expensive belief POLARIS is currently holding that this research overturns:** the operator-locked DeepSeek V4 Pro generator is the WRONG generator for the hallucination-sensitivity dimension. Vectara HHEM-2.3 evaluator (May 2026 leaderboard) ranks V4 Pro at 8.6%, V3.2-Exp at 5.3%, Qwen3-8B at 4.8%, Antgroup Finix S1 32B at 1.8%. All four lower-hallucination options are open-weight non-US. V4 Pro is also notorious for 94% non-abstention on AA-Omniscience (it answers when it doesn't know). The 44.4% refusal rate POLARIS is seeing is V4 Pro's low-abstention pathology being correctly caught by POLARIS's validator. This is a generator-choice problem, not a validator problem. **Per CLAUDE.md §-1.1 + `feedback_route_policy_questions_to_codex.md` I am surfacing this for Codex review, not silently switching.** Operator's "frontier-only, top-notch only" directive was a general capability directive; the dimension-specific data argues for an additional consideration. Most defensible path: keep V4 Pro as primary reasoning generator AND add Qwen3-8B as a parallel "low-hallucination prose finalizer" model in the same ensemble used by CHECK, exploiting the family diversity that POLARIS's two-family invariant (§9.1) already requires.
docs\clinical_rag_sota_deepest_research_2026_05_27.md:48:2. **No log-probability / information-theoretic anomaly detector across the generator ensemble.** This is the gap the CHECK paper [^check_paper] specifically addresses: hallucinations have **statistically distinct distribution shapes** (high entropy, high KL divergence across models in an ensemble) that are detectable without any retrieval. POLARIS's two-family invariant already requires two distinct LLM lineages — extending to 3-5 and computing entropy / KL across them is mechanical. **Fix:** re-implement CHECK's classifier on POLARIS's existing generator + a Qwen3 mirror + a Llama-3.3 mirror.
docs\clinical_rag_sota_deepest_research_2026_05_27.md:50:3. **No per-claim atomic decomposition + retrieval check post-generation.** This is the P-Cite layer the literature recommends as the dominant pattern [^gcite_pcite]. POLARIS does G-Cite (pre-generation atom anchoring) but doesn't currently decompose the GENERATED text into atomic claims and re-verify each against retrieved evidence. The G-Cite scaffold misses claims that emerge in narrative connective tissue (the "did not lead to" pattern). **Fix:** MedRAGChecker pattern with Qwen-derived student extractors + biomedical KG.

1: # Verifier self-host serving runbook — I-meta-002 PR-8 / M2
2: 
3: > **⚠ PAID STEP — the one-question canary. NOT executed by this PR.**
4: > Every command below rents real Vast.ai GPU boxes and bills per second. Nothing
5: > in PR-8/M2 runs these steps. They are the exact operator procedure for the
6: > later, operator-triggered, single-question canary deploy. Until the operator
7: > explicitly launches the canary, the three boxes do NOT exist and `PG_*_BASE_URL`
8: > are unset.
9: 
10: ## What this runbook does
11: 
12: Brings up the **three self-hosted verifier roles** on Vast.ai, each serving the
13: EXACT model pinned in `config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml`, then runs
14: the offline-tested identity probe (`scripts/dr_benchmark/verify_serving_identity.py`)
15: against the live boxes to confirm `served == pinned` before any benchmark question
16: is asked.
17: 
18: The **generator (DeepSeek V4 Pro) is NOT here** — it stays on OpenRouter
19: (`serving_route: openrouter`) across every benchmark scenario. Only Mirror,
20: Sentinel, and Judge are self-hosted.
21: 
22: Per-role serving spec is declared in `config/serving/verifier_roles.yaml`. This
23: runbook is the human procedure that realizes that config on rented hardware.
24: 
25: ## Roles, boxes, and launch args (from `config/serving/verifier_roles.yaml`)
26: 
27: - **Mirror** — `cohere/command-a-plus`, bf16 full precision, **8×H100** box
28:   (~438 GB weights). Plain chat serving (no structured-output constraint) so the
29:   model emits inline `<co>covered text</co:doc_id>` citation spans.
30: - **Sentinel** — `ibm-granite/granite-guardian-4.1-8b`, **1×A100 80GB** box
31:   (dense ~16 GB). Plain chat (emits a `<score>` element, not JSON).
32: - **Judge** — `qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b`, fp8, **1×H100** box (~35 GB). Structured /
33:   guided decoding ENABLED so the verdict is hard-enum constrained at decode time.
34: 
35: > The cost arithmetic lives in `docs/vast_ai_budget_i_meta_002.md`. For PR-8/M2
36: > and the one-question canary, the FULL-SELF-HOST path is the ONLY valid path: all
37: > three verifier roles (Mirror, Sentinel, Judge) MUST be served from rented Vast
38: > boxes. Do NOT point ANY verifier role's `PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL` at a managed-vendor
39: > endpoint (Cohere's hosted API or any other). A managed-vendor Mirror endpoint
40: > breaks BOTH the architecture lock (Mirror `serving_route: vast_self_host_bf16`)
41: > AND the sovereignty threat model (NO runtime US-vendor LLM calls; Cohere's
42: > managed API is a US vendor). The identity probe only checks the SERVED slug, so a
43: > managed endpoint advertising the right name would PASS the probe while running the
44: > wrong, non-sovereign architecture — the probe cannot catch this, so the operator
45: > must NOT introduce a managed endpoint in the first place.
46: 
47: ## Step 0 — preconditions (operator)
48: 
49: 1. Vast.ai account funded (see `docs/vast_ai_budget_i_meta_002.md` for the credit
50:    to load per scenario).
51: 2. `vastai` CLI installed and logged in (`vastai set api-key <KEY>`).
52: 3. An SSH key registered with Vast for box access.
53: 4. **Sovereignty note:** Vast H100/H200 inventory is US-only — acceptable ONLY
54:    because the DRB-EN benchmark uses PUBLIC data, no clinical data. Do NOT serve
55:    clinical/patient data from a Vast box (production clinical = direct-EU
56:    procurement, separate decision — `docs/vast_ai_budget_i_meta_002.md` §4).
57: 
58: ## Step 1 — rent the three boxes (PAID)
59: 
60: Search for offers, then `vastai create instance` per role. Example shape (replace
61: `<offer_id>` with a matching offer from `vastai search offers`):
62: 
63: ```bash
64: # Mirror — 8xH100 bf16
65: vastai search offers 'num_gpus=8 gpu_name=H100 inet_down>1000' --order 'dph'
66: vastai create instance <mirror_offer_id> \
67:   --image vllm/vllm-openai:latest \
68:   --disk 600 \
69:   --env '-p 8000:8000'
70: 
71: # Sentinel — 1xA100 80GB
72: vastai search offers 'num_gpus=1 gpu_name=A100_SXM4 gpu_ram>=80'
73: vastai create instance <sentinel_offer_id> \
74:   --image vllm/vllm-openai:latest \
75:   --disk 60 \
76:   --env '-p 8000:8000'
77: 
78: # Judge — 1xH100 fp8
79: vastai search offers 'num_gpus=1 gpu_name=H100'
80: vastai create instance <judge_offer_id> \
81:   --image vllm/vllm-openai:latest \
82:   --disk 80 \
83:   --env '-p 8000:8000'
84: ```
85: 
86: > **Storage bills even when STOPPED — only DESTROY halts it.** Per §8.4
87: > resource discipline + `docs/vast_ai_budget_i_meta_002.md` §0.3, the dominant
88: > cost is hold-hours. Destroy each box the moment its session ends.
89: 
90: ## Step 2 — launch vLLM per role (PAID, on each box)

3: # Declarative, per-role vLLM serving spec for the THREE self-hosted verifier
4: # roles (mirror / sentinel / judge). The generator is NOT here: it runs live on
5: # OpenRouter (serving_route: openrouter in the runtime lock) and is upstream of
6: # the per-claim verifier path.
7: #
8: # SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for slugs: config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml.
9: #   - `model_slug` below MUST equal the lock's required_roles.<role>.model_slug.
10: #   - `vllm_args.served_model_name` MUST equal that same slug — this is the
11: #     identity the M4 served==pinned gate trusts and the identity probe
12: #     (scripts/dr_benchmark/verify_serving_identity.py) asserts against /v1/models.
13: #   - The probe + the config-shape test fail loud if any role drifts from the lock.
14: #
15: # Codex brief-gate ruling (P2, 2026-05-29): keep `model_source` (the HF repo /
16: # local path vLLM LOADS) DISTINCT from `served_model_name` (the public id vLLM
17: # ADVERTISES). Only `served_model_name` must equal the lock slug; `model_source`
18: # is the weights location and is intentionally free of the lock-equality rule.
19: #
20: # NO SECRETS in this file (LAW VI): API keys arrive only via the per-role

142: def test_serving_config_declares_model_source_distinct_from_served_name(_expected_slugs):
143:     """Each role carries a model_source (the weights path); only served_model_name
144:     is bound to the lock slug. model_source is NOT lock-equality checked, so it may
145:     legitimately equal OR differ from the served slug (the casing/path differs in
146:     practice, e.g. CohereLabs/command-a-plus-05-2026-bf16 vs cohere/command-a-plus).
147:     """
148:     config = _load_serving_config()
149:     for role in SELF_HOST_ROLES:
150:         block = config["roles"][role]
151:         # model_source MUST be present and a non-empty string (the weights path).
152:         assert "model_source" in block, role
153:         assert isinstance(block["model_source"], str) and block["model_source"].strip(), role
154:         # served_model_name remains the lock-bound id; model_source is independent of it
155:         # (no lock-equality assertion on model_source — it is the weights location only).
156:         assert block["vllm_args"]["served_model_name"] == _expected_slugs[role], role

Checked evidence:
- Ledger sum: per-run ledger and `logs/pg_cost_ledger.jsonl` filtered to `SWEEP_clinical_clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm_1779855481` both give 138 entries, `$0.737067` generate + `$0.010619` entailment judge = `$0.747686`.
- Manifest undercount: `manifest.json:33` has `$0.064110928`; `openrouter_client.py` uses `ContextVar` run-cost accumulation, and `run_honest_sweep_r3.py` writes that parent-context value into `cost_usd`.
- GPU topology: true for the full locked verifier cluster, not for Command A+ alone. Command A+ BF16 is 218B total / 25B active and Hugging Face lists BF16 minimum as `8 x H100`; repo adds Qwen judge `1 x H100` and Granite sentinel `1 x A100`, so full cluster is `9 x H100 + 1 x A100`. Source: https://huggingface.co/CohereLabs/command-a-plus-05-2026-bf16
- DeepSeek V4 Pro repo price matches OpenRouter `$0.435/M` input and `$0.87/M` output. Source: https://openrouter.ai/deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro
- Vast pricing is live marketplace pricing, not a stable fixed tariff. Source: https://vast.ai/pricing and current public listings at https://gpu.fund/
- ClinicalTrials.gov is not wired into the shipped clinical backend; `domain_backends.py:452-453` marks it as a 403/future fast-follow, and the clinical dispatcher runs Europe PMC instead.
